understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to maneuver selectable pieces onto goal tiles marked '+' or satisfy the engine's WIN condition within a finite step budget per level, progressing through all levels to win the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Pieces are selected (possibly by initial CLICK or persistent selection). MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN moves the selected piece one step in that cardinal direction if not blocked. CLICK [x,y] attempts to move or teleport the currently selected piece directly to the target [x,y] position if unblocked/clear, otherwise no change ('blocked or no movement effect'). Walls and obstacles block movements. No pushing mechanics observed in recent turns; movements are direct on selected piece.

orient_history: Puzzle game like sokoban with multiple selectables as pushable pieces. Select a piece via CLICK on its position, then MOVE it (arrows) to push/place on goals. Pieces and walls are collidable; goals non-collidable. Pixels: 3px=1 cell. Win level by placing pieces on all goals.

Game is a puzzle with multiple selectable pieces that act as pushables or movable objects. CLICK on a position: if empty, moves current selected piece there; if on a selectable, selects/deselects it as the new player. MOVE_[DIR] attempts to move the currently selected piece one step (3 pixels/cell) in that direction if target not blocked/collidable. Walls block movement. Success by placing selectables on all goal positions (2 pieces, 2 goals). Grid in cells (3 pixels/cell), borders walled. No blocks present.

The game involves maneuvering selectable pieces (pushable objects) onto goal tiles. There are two identical selectables; one is always selected (player-controlled). CLICK on another selectable's position swaps the selected piece there, updating selection. Directional MOVE actions are blocked by walls or goals. CLICK allows teleporting the selected piece to specific positions (at least other selectables'; likely goals too). Goals are non-collidable. Success requires selectables covering both goals. Swapping alone traps pieces in narrow walled chambers (P##S). Must use CLICK on goal coords to place piece.

Selectable pieces are the pushable objects (like boxes) that need to be maneuvered onto goal tiles (+). CLICK on a selectable's position switches selection to it (updating the 'player' to that piece). CLICK on non-selectable positions attempts to relocate the current selected piece there but fails if blocked (e.g., goals or obstacles). MOVE_[DIR] slides the selected piece one step in that direction if unblocked. Current piece at cell (3,7) is trapped by walls and possibly goal adjacency. The other piece at cell (6,7) may have more freedom towards the right goal at [35,17]. Two pieces for two goals. Large border wall and internal walls constrain movement.
